highlights: Convert DD, DMS, UTM, and MGRS on WGS84
Vincenty geodesic distance, bearing, and direct
Offline map measure — path length and polygon area
Waypoints with GPX 1.1 export — no account required

converter.title: Multi-format converter
converter.description: Convert between Decimal Degrees, DMS, UTM, and MGRS on the WGS84 ellipsoid. Auto-detect format or pick decimal order.

distance.title: Geodesic distance & bearing
distance.description: Ellipsoidal distance and forward azimuth between two points using Vincenty inverse — not flat-map shortcuts.

map.title: Map measure
map.description: Draw paths and polygons on an offline map canvas. Measure path length and polygon area on the WGS84 ellipsoid.

waypoints.title: Waypoints & GPX
waypoints.description: Save named waypoints locally. Export waypoints or track vertices as GPX 1.1 for GIS and GPS devices.

offline.title: Offline by design
offline.description: Core conversion, distance, and map measure work without a network. No sign-up — waypoints stay on your device.

languages.title: 12 languages
languages.description: Field teams worldwide — including Hebrew and Arabic with RTL UI support.

manual.title: User manual
manual.subtitle: Converter, distance, map, waypoints, and settings.

sections.title: 1. Coordinate converter
steps: Paste or type a coordinate string — DD, DMS, UTM, or MGRS.
Toggle auto-detect or pick input format manually.
Set coordinate order (lat/lng or lng/lat) in Settings if needed.
Tap copy icons to send results to another app.

sections.title: 2. Distance & bearing
steps: Enter point A and point B in DD or DMS.
Read geodesic distance and forward azimuth.
Switch metric, imperial, or nautical units in Settings.
Use Direct mode to find a point given start, distance, and bearing.

sections.title: 3. Map measure
steps: Tap to add vertices along a path or polygon.
Read path length or polygon area on WGS84.
Allow location only if you want GPS map centering.

sections.title: 4. Waypoints & GPX
steps: Save points from converter or map with a name.
Browse the local waypoint list — no cloud sync.
Export selected waypoints or track as GPX 1.1.
Share GPX via Files, email, or GIS apps.

tips.title: Tips & tricks
tips.subtitle: Get reliable results in the field.

items.title: Verify critical work
items.description: GEO Calc uses standard WGS84 geodesy — not a substitute for certified survey instruments or legal boundaries.

items.title: No network needed
items.description: Download offline map tiles before heading to remote sites if your build includes map caching.

items.title: Coordinate order
items.description: Set lat/lng vs lng/lat once in Settings to avoid swapped results on international projects.

items.title: GPX handoff
items.description: Export waypoints as GPX and open in QGIS, Google Earth, or a handheld GPS unit.

items.title: Unit systems
items.description: Switch metric, imperial, or nautical for distances and areas — useful for marine and aviation cross-checks.

items.title: Optional GPS
items.description: Converter and distance tools work from typed coordinates — GPS is only for map centering.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Offline use, accuracy, ads, and platforms.

items.question: What is GEO Calc?
items.answer: GEO Calc is an offline WGS84 coordinate calculator for iOS and Android. Convert DD, DMS, UTM, and MGRS; compute Vincenty distance and bearing; measure on a map; export GPX. Android package: com.zuki.apps.geocalc.

items.question: Does GEO Calc need internet?
items.answer: Core conversion, distance, and map measure work offline. Ads and analytics may use the network when available.

items.question: Is it survey-grade?
items.answer: Calculations use standard WGS84 geodesy (Vincenty, ellipsoidal area). Verify critical measurements with certified survey equipment — GEO Calc is a field reference tool, not legal boundary authority.

items.question: Do I need an account?
items.answer: No. Waypoints and preferences are stored on your device only.

items.question: Is there a subscription?
items.answer: No. GEO Calc v1.0 is free with optional banner and interstitial ads. No in-app purchases at launch.

items.question: Does it use my GPS location?
items.answer: Location is optional — used when you open map or GPS features. Converter and distance tools work from typed coordinates alone.

items.question: What coordinate formats are supported?
items.answer: Decimal Degrees (DD), Degrees-Minutes-Seconds (DMS), UTM, and MGRS on WGS84.

items.question: Can I export to GIS?
items.answer: Yes — save waypoints and export GPX 1.1 for QGIS, ArcGIS, Google Earth, and GPS devices.

items.question: Which languages are supported?
items.answer: 12 languages: English, Hebrew, Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Chinese (Simplified).

items.question: How do I get support?
items.answer: Email zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com or visit https://zukiapps.com/geo-calc/support. Privacy: https://zukiapps.com/geo-calc/privacy

languages.title: 12 languages

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: How GEO Calc handles your data

q1.question: How do I convert UTM to decimal degrees?
q1.answer: Open Coordinate Converter, paste your UTM string, and read DD (and DMS/MGRS) in the results panel. Works offline.

q2.question: Why is my distance different from a flat map?
q2.answer: GEO Calc uses Vincenty geodesic distance on the WGS84 ellipsoid. Flat or Web Mercator rulers underestimate long spans — especially east-west at high latitude.

q3.question: Can I use GEO Calc without GPS?
q3.answer: Yes. Converter and distance tools only need typed coordinates. GPS is optional for centering the map.

q4.question: How do I export GPX?
q4.answer: Save waypoints from the app, open the Waypoints screen, select items, and tap Export GPX. Share the file via the system sheet.

q5.question: Are waypoints backed up to the cloud?
q5.answer: No. Waypoints and preferences are stored locally. Export GPX if you need a backup.

q6.question: Where is the full FAQ?
q6.answer: See the FAQ on the GEO Calc product page or read public/geo-calc/faq.md on zukiapps.com for answer-engine friendly copy.

additionalHelp.title: Need more help?
additionalHelp.description: If you could not find an answer, email zuki.apps.dev@gmail.com. We typically respond within 24–48 hours.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDescription: Privacy Policy for GEO Calc — local waypoint storage, optional location, ads and analytics SDKs, no account required.
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Zuki Apps (“we”, “us”) handles information when you use the GEO Calc mobile application and related pages at zukiapps.com/geo-calc (the “Service”). GEO Calc stores waypoints and preferences on your device. We do not require an account.

section1.title: 1. Who we are
section1.content: The data controller for GEO Calc is Zuki Apps. Contact details are at the end of this Policy.
section1.content2: This Policy applies to the GEO Calc app (iOS and Android) and the legal pages we publish for the product.

section2.title: 2. Information we collect
section2.content: Waypoint names, coordinates you enter, and app settings are stored locally on your device and are not transmitted to our servers.
items: Waypoint & coordinate content: local only — not collected on our servers
Coarse/precise location (optional): used for map/GPS features when you grant permission
Device or advertising IDs (AdMob): may be used for ads in the free version
App interaction and crash data (Firebase Analytics / Crashlytics): aggregated diagnostics
Support email: if you contact us directly

section3.title: 3. How we use information
items: Provide coordinate conversion, distance, map measure, and GPX export
Optionally center the map on your location when permitted
Show ads in the free tier (Google AdMob)
Measure app stability and usage via analytics SDKs
section3.content2: We do not sell your personal information. We do not upload your waypoints or typed coordinates to our servers.

section4.title: 4. Location
section4.content: GEO Calc may request location permission for map centering and optional GPS features. Converter and distance tools work without location access.
section4.content2: You can revoke location permission in your device settings at any time.

section5.title: 5. Storage and retention
section5.content: Waypoint data remains on your device until you delete it or uninstall the app. We do not operate a cloud account for waypoint sync in v1.0.
section5.content2: Support emails are retained as long as needed to resolve your request.

section6.title: 6. Advertising and analytics
section6.content: The free version may show banner and interstitial ads via Google AdMob. Firebase Analytics and Crashlytics may collect limited device and usage data as described in Google’s policies.
section6.content2: On iOS, the app may request App Tracking Transparency permission for personalized ads.

section7.title: 7. Your rights and children
section7.content: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or object to certain processing. Contact us to exercise rights.
section7.content2: GEO Calc is not directed to children under 13 (or the minimum age in your country). We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.

section8.title: 8. Changes
section8.content: We may update this Policy from time to time. We will post the revised version with a new “Last updated” date.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.metaDescription: Terms of Use for GEO Calc — coordinate calculator, field reference tool disclaimers, and acceptable use.
terms.intro: Please read these Terms of Use carefully before using GEO Calc. By downloading, installing, or using the app, you agree to these Terms and our Privacy Policy.

section1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
section1.content: These Terms govern your use of GEO Calc (“Service”, “App”) operated by Zuki Apps (“we”, “us”). If you do not agree, do not use the Service.
section1.content2: If you use the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent that you have authority to bind that organization.

section2.title: 2. Description of Service
section2.content: GEO Calc is a WGS84 coordinate and geodesy reference app. The Service includes:
items: Vincenty geodesic distance, bearing, and direct
Offline map measure — path and polygon area

section3.title: 3. Not professional surveying advice
section3.content: GEO Calc is a field reference tool only. It is not a certified surveying instrument and does not determine legal property boundaries.
section3.content2: Verify critical measurements and boundary work with qualified professionals and appropriate certified equipment.

section4.title: 4. Free service and ads
section4.content: GEO Calc v1.0 is free with optional banner and interstitial ads. There is no subscription at launch.
section4.content2: Features and ad placement may change with notice where required by law.

section5.title: 5. Acceptable use
items: Reverse engineer the app except as permitted by law
Use the Service for unlawful purposes
Rely on the app as sole authority for legal boundary or safety-critical decisions
Interfere with ads, analytics, or security features

section6.title: 6. Disclaimer
section6.content: The Service is provided “as is” without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free operation or fitness for a particular surveying purpose.
section6.content2: You are responsible for backing up waypoints you care about (e.g. via GPX export).

section7.title: 7. Limitation of liability
section7.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Zuki Apps shall not be liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from use of the Service, including reliance on coordinate or area calculations.

section8.title: 8. Changes
section8.content: We may modify these Terms. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance.
